page: Amazon Web Services c8i.32xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 128 virtual CPUs and 64 physical cores based on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors running at 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, but it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place this instance in the top tier for multi-core CPU execution, as demonstrated by stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core scores. The Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions, which contribute to top-tier performance in Passmark extended instructions and database operations. Memory performance is also highly rated, with cached and uncached read-only memory bandwidth benchmarks reaching the top tier.

The instance demonstrates strong capabilities in specialized workloads, achieving top-tier results in Redis SET operations, static web serving throughput, and LLM inference prompt processing across small, medium, and large models. However, text generation for smaller models falls into the average category. Given its high compute-to-memory ratio and lack of local storage, this instance is highly cost-efficient for compute-bound workloads such as software compilation, ray tracing, and high-throughput database operations that rely on network-attached storage.
description: Amazon Web Services c8i.32xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors running at 3.9 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it includes 256.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 7 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core, with no local storage or GPUs. The instance delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operation speeds, supported by hardware acceleration features like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. It is optimized for high-throughput network operations with a 50 Gbps baseline bandwidth. This instance is best suited for compute-intensive workloads, including software compilation, large-scale database operations, and LLM prompt processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.32xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 128 vCPUs, Intel Xeon processors, and 256 GB of DDR5 RAM, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for intensive workloads.

tagline: A high-core-density compute instance featuring DDR5 memory and advanced hardware acceleration for demanding multi-threaded processing and database workloads.

256.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance

50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPUs
